QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $713.13 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Friday's session closed sharply lower as the Trump-Xi summit ended without a trade deal, dashing hopes for chip-export relief. The S&P 500 fell 0.8% to 741.84, while the NASDAQ-100 lost 1% with QQQ at 713.13. Oil rose another 2.4% intraday to $104.03 before settling at $101.16 after fresh ship seizures in the Strait of Hormuz and a drone-damaged tanker — elevating supply fears and keeping inflation expectations elevated. Bonds sold off with the US 10-year yield climbing 14bp to 4.60% as Fed officials warned inflation is the 'most pressing risk' and markets priced the Fed behind the curve.
commodities_note: WTI closed at $101.16, up 0.21% after hitting a session high of $104.03. Gold slipped 1.3% to $4,561.20. The US dollar (DXY) firmed 0.25% to 99.29. QQQ data shows a potential anomaly: the price at $713.13 appears far above historical norms — verify data source for possible contract adjustment.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks are hard-blocked on technical extension and RSI, with US10Y at 4.60% — well above the 4.20% threshold where NIM compression eases. The cluster is structurally impaired: even the best performer (DBS at +7.0% above SMA50, RSI 81) offers a negative R:R. No entry is viable until rates soften or a pullback resets the charts. Intra-cluster divergence is minimal.
singapore.key_development: US10Y rose 14bp to 4.60%, extending the dual-channel NIM squeeze for Singapore banks; no relief expected before MAS's October policy review.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster sold off sharply on Friday with SOXX down 2.6% to 516.58, as the Trump-Xi no-deal and TSM's share sale plan spooked investors. Every name remains hard-blocked by extreme extension (AMD +55%, INTC +58%, MU +52%, SK Hynix +57%) or RSI; no off-ramp is visible. Cluster posture is uniformly CAUTION with all members in avoid territory.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically broken — extreme extension blocks all entry
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX dropped 2.6% versus a 0.8% SPY decline; the sell-off was led by memory names (MU -5.2%, SK Hynix -7.7%) after Trump-Xi failed to deliver chip-export concessions.

big_tech.summary: MSFT surged 4.2% on Bill Ackman's stake disclosure and Azure RPO hope, but the rally pushed it to a hard-blocked +6.6% above SMA50. AMZN and GOOG remain extended above 11% and 19% respectively; all are CAUTION. PLTR is the lone name trading below its 50-day but with a punishing 65x forward PE. The cluster is a valuation trap — no entry without a growth catalyst that justifies current multiples.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally cautious — extreme valuation with no technical entry
big_tech.key_development: MSFT jumped 4.2% on Ackman's position, but the move triggered a mechanical extension block, leaving the entire cluster still off-limits.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking upgrades directly benefit Arista's switch revenue — a read-through on Nokia's AI networking thesis and hyperscaler capex.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if ANET pulls back 8-10% from current levels; tied to NOK and NVDA networking demand.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment includes Coherent alongside Lumentum — both partner on AI optical interconnects and benefit from LITE's capacity buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add on a pullback >10% from current levels; NVDA photonics ramp expected to accelerate into 2027.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Samsung's labor strike threatens HBM supply, tightening the memory duopoly just as SK Hynix and Micron extend — any HBM output disruption directly lifts MU and SK Hynix pricing.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor after May 21 strike deadline; add on a 15% pullback from current levels if HBM production stabilizes.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz crisis: ship attacks and vessel sinkings continue, with daily crossings still well below pre-war levels; oil supply shock fuels inflation.
Iran conflict escalation: ceasefire hopes dashed after Trump's rejection of Iranian proposal; US military posture and drone strikes raise further disruption risk.
US-China trade uncertainty: Trump-Xi summit produced no chip-export deal, managed tariff cuts on $30B imports still unclear; semiconductor supply chain uncertainty persists.
Fed inflation fight: April CPI at 3.8% and hawkish Fed rhetoric (Schmid: 'most pressing risk') under new chair Warsh suggest rate hikes could resume.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $101.16 keeps inflation expectations elevated, pushing US10Y to 4.60% and strengthening the dollar — a headwind for Singapore banks. Semis and BigTech remain under pressure as no trade deal materialised, while defense and energy-linked names like PLTR and BE stay in risk-off mode.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump-Xi summit concluded with 'fantastic trade deals' rhetoric but no specifics on semiconductor export curbs. Oil rose 2.4% intraday after Trump and Iran comments dashed peace hopes, then settled only slightly higher.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Stay in cash — 21 of 22 watchlist names are hard-blocked by extension or RSI; only WRD offers a borderline entry as a starter position.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — uniformly extended and no actionable entries
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Twenty-one of 22 names are hard-blocked on technical extension or RSI — the entire watchlist is in avoid or wait mode. The only exception is WRD (WATCH), a small-cap ADR with a fragile single-leg thesis.
